summary: Add support for system-generated turns — external events (file watchers, CI callbacks, MCP notifications, webhooks) that automatically trigger a new model turn in a provider session without explicit user input. Introduces a new 'system-event' message role in the orchestration layer, with per-adapter mapping to each provider's best available mechanism. Enables autonomous agent workflows, background monitoring, and event-driven AI responses.

compatibility.reasoning: The orchestration layer is fully event-sourced with a command/event/decider pattern. Adding a new command type (thread.turn.start-system) alongside the existing user-initiated turn commands is a natural extension. The OrchestrationMessageRole union already includes 'system' (unused), and adding 'system-event' is trivial. Provider sessions are persistent between turns, so ProviderService.sendTurn() can be called without a new session. The ProviderCommandReactor and RuntimeReceiptBus provide the event infrastructure needed. No existing flows are affected.

affected_areas: packages/contracts/src/orchestration.ts - Add 'system-event' to OrchestrationMessageRole, add source/triggerRef fields to OrchestrationMessage
apps/server/src/orchestration/ - New command type thread.turn.start-system in deciders, new events for system-initiated turns
apps/server/src/provider/ProviderCommandReactor.ts - Extend to handle system-initiated turn-start-requested events
apps/server/src/provider/Layers/ClaudeAdapter.ts - Map system-event role to SDKUserMessage with isSynthetic: true
apps/server/src/provider/Layers/CopilotAdapter.ts - Map system-event role to prompt string with [System Event] prefix
apps/server/src/provider/Layers/CodexAdapter.ts - Map system-event role to developer_instructions per-turn
apps/server/src/provider/Layers/OpenCodeAdapter.ts - Map system-event role to native system field on SessionPromptAsyncData
apps/web/src/components/MessagesTimeline.tsx - Render system-event messages with distinct styling (collapsible, different icon/color)
apps/web/src/ - New TriggerRuleManager UI (parallel to ProjectScriptsControl pattern for '+ Add trigger')
apps/server/src/ - New EventTriggerService for managing trigger sources and dispatching system turns
acceptance_criteria: A new 'system-event' role exists in OrchestrationMessageRole and is distinct from 'system' and 'user'
System-event messages can be injected into a thread and trigger a provider turn without user action
Each provider adapter maps system-event to its best available mechanism (Claude: isSynthetic user, Copilot: prefixed prompt, Codex: developer_instructions, OpenCode: native system field)
System-event messages render distinctly in the UI (different visual treatment from user/assistant messages)
A policy layer can block system-event turns pending user approval (opt-in gating)
Rate limiting prevents runaway loops (model response triggers file change triggers another turn)
Cost attribution separates user-initiated vs system-initiated token spend
Compaction/projection retains system-event messages (extend existing system message retention logic)
Existing user-initiated turn flows are completely unaffected
implementation_notes: Option B (new role) chosen over Option A (map to user) for semantic clarity, UI differentiation, and policy/blocking support
Option C (tool call/result encoding) was investigated and rejected — SDKs don't allow fabricating assistant tool_use blocks, and only Claude has tool-level APIs; other SDKs are opaque
Claude SDK: use SDKUserMessage { isSynthetic: true, shouldQuery: false, priority: 'now' } for context injection, or with shouldQuery: true to trigger a turn
OpenCode SDK has a native system field on promptAsync that the adapter doesn't use yet — lowest friction path
Codex SDK supports per-turn developer_instructions via collaborationMode.settings — good semantic fit
Copilot SDK is most limited — string prompt only, no role control; prepend [System Event] marker to prompt
MCP's sampling/createMessage is the only standardized protocol for server-initiated model invocation — could serve as a trigger source
The '+ Add action' button (ProjectScriptsControl) is a UX precedent for an '+ Add trigger' feature
Event sources to support: file watchers (fs.watch), MCP notifications (resources/updated), webhooks (HTTP endpoint), git hooks, CI callbacks
unresolved_questions: Should system-event turns require explicit user approval by default, or be opt-in to auto-mode?
What is the rate limit / budget policy for auto-triggered turns? Per-minute? Per-session token cap?
Should system-event messages appear inline in the chat timeline or in a separate panel/sidebar?
How should loop detection work — track event→turn→event chains and break at depth N?
Should triggers be scoped per-thread or global across all threads?
Should we support 'quiet' system events that inject context without triggering a turn (using Claude's shouldQuery: false)?
How does this interact with the existing hook system (PreToolUse, PostToolUse) — are hooks a trigger source?
Should the feature support conditional triggers (only fire if pattern matches, file is in scope, etc.)?
